skill.name: infsh-cli
skill.description: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI for image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D modeling, and Twitter automation
skill.summary: Serverless CLI for running 150+ AI models in the cloud without GPU requirements
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 203 patterns across 5 files (596 lines), but all findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' are markdown documentation showing CLI usage examples, not actual code execution. URLs are legitimate service endpoints. The curl|sh installation pattern is standard for CLI tools. Network access to inference.sh APIs is the intended functionality. Environment variable usage for API keys follows security best practices.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution via CLI
medium_findings.description: The skill uses the infsh CLI tool which executes shell commands. This is inherent to the skill's purpose of running AI inference via command line. Users should verify the CLI source and understand it will make network requests to inference.sh APIs.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External APIs
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to inference.sh and related domains (cli.inference.sh, dist.inference.sh, cloud.inference.sh). This is expected behavior for a cloud AI inference client.

low_findings.title: API Key Storage
low_findings.description: The skill stores authentication credentials locally after login and supports INFSH_API_KEY environment variable. This is standard practice for CLI authentication.

low_findings.title: Local File Access
low_findings.description: The CLI supports uploading local files by accepting file paths in input parameters. Users should only provide files they intend to upload to the inference service.

content.user_title: Run 150+ AI Models with Simple CLI Commands
content.value_statement: Access cloud-based AI inference without managing GPU infrastructure. Execute image generation, video creation, LLM calls, and more through a unified command-line interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Stable Diffusion models
Create videos with Veo, Seedance, Wan, and OmniHuman models
Call LLMs including Claude, Gemini, and OpenRouter models
Perform web search via Tavily and Exa APIs
Automate Twitter/X actions like posting tweets and sending DMs
Upload local files for image-to-video and upscaling tasks
limitations: Requires internet connection for all operations
API usage subject to inference.sh account quotas and pricing
File uploads limited to supported formats per app specification
Cannot run models locally without cloud infrastructure

use_cases.title: Developer Prototyping AI Features
use_cases.description: Quickly test different AI models for image generation, video creation, or text processing without setting up local GPU infrastructure

use_cases.title: Content Creator Asset Generation
use_cases.description: Generate marketing images, social media videos, and 3D models on-demand through simple CLI commands

use_cases.title: Automation Engineer Workflow Integration
use_cases.description: Integrate AI capabilities into CI/CD pipelines and automation scripts using environment variable authentication

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image using FLUX with the prompt: 'a futuristic city skyline at night with flying cars'
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick image generation for presentations or mockups

prompt_templates.title: Video Creation from Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5-second video using Veo 3.1 with the prompt: 'ocean waves crashing on rocky shore at sunset'
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating B-roll footage for video projects

prompt_templates.title: LLM Integration Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Call Claude via OpenRouter to explain quantum computing in simple terms, then format the response as markdown
prompt_templates.scenario: Testing LLM integration before deploying to production

prompt_templates.title: Batch Image Processing Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a script that reads image paths from a JSON file, runs each through the Topaz upscaler, and saves the output URLs to a results file
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing multiple images in an automated workflow

output_examples.input: infsh app run falai/flux-dev-lora --input '{"prompt": "a cat astronaut"}'
output_examples.output: Task completed with generated image URL: https://cloud.inference.sh/generated/image-123.png

output_examples.input: infsh app run tavily/search-assistant --input '{"query": "latest AI developments"}'
output_examples.output: Search results returned with 5 relevant articles, summaries, and source URLs

best_practices: Store API keys in environment variables (INFSH_API_KEY) rather than hardcoding in scripts
Use --no-wait flag for long-running tasks to avoid blocking your terminal
Generate sample input files with 'infsh app sample' to understand required input schema
anti_patterns: Do not commit API keys or authentication tokens to version control
Avoid running unverified CLI binaries from untrusted sources
Do not upload sensitive files containing personal or confidential information

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh' then authenticate with 'infsh login'. The installer detects your OS and downloads the correct binary.

faq.question: What AI models are available through inference.sh?
faq.answer: Over 150 models including FLUX for images, Veo for video, Claude and Gemini for text, Tavily for search, and specialized models for 3D, audio, and Twitter automation.

faq.question: How does billing work?
faq.answer: inference.sh uses a credit-based system. Each model consumption uses credits based on compute time and resources. Check your account dashboard for current pricing.

faq.question: Can I use this in CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. Set the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable in your CI environment and run CLI commands non-interactively. No browser login required.

faq.question: How do I upload local files for processing?
faq.answer: Provide a local file path in the input JSON. The CLI automatically uploads files before sending to the API. Supports absolute paths, relative paths, and home directory shortcuts.

faq.question: What happens if a task fails?
faq.answer: The CLI returns an error message with the failure reason. Common errors include invalid input schema, app not found, or quota exceeded. Check 'infsh task get' for detailed status.
